complexity_analysis.claimed_benefit: Quick Java implementation with simplicity focus

test_results.test_scenario: Simple: Create a basic Java method
issues_identified: Must read through 250 lines before getting to actual implementation
3-Question Rule creates decision paralysis
Complexity scoring system more complex than most actual Java problems
Delegates to OTHER commands for complexity > 3 (defeats purpose)
Immediately delegates to java-clean-implementer
User gets bounce-back experience
No actual rapid implementation happens

savage_analysis.verdict: Claims rapid implementation but forces users through a 250-line philosophical journey about complexity scoring. The command that promises speed creates more overhead than writing the Java code manually.

complexity_analysis.claimed_benefit: Intelligent hyperfocus management with pattern-based boundaries

test_results.test_scenario: Start hyperfocus session for coding task
issues_identified: Requires 3 minutes of setup to start a productivity session
47-step checklist more complex than most ADHD brains can handle
Paradox: Tool to help focus requires massive focus to use

savage_analysis.verdict: A 457-line command that claims to help ADHD focus while being the most unfocused, scattered command in existence. It's like designing a meditation app that requires reading War and Peace first.

complexity_analysis.claimed_benefit: Ultra-deep thinking logic with interactive clarification

issues_identified: Forces user through 5 clarifying questions before starting
Multi-layer thinking creates analysis paralysis
Most thinking happens in meta-levels that don't add value

savage_analysis.verdict: Promises ultra-deep thinking but delivers ultra-deep procrastination. Spends more tokens thinking about thinking than actually thinking.

complexity_analysis.claimed_benefit: Persistent refactoring with validation and rollback

test_results.test_scenario: Simple: Rename a function across project
issues_identified: 534 lines of documentation for what could be a find-and-replace
Creates session files, plans, states - more complex than refactoring itself
Six phases for changing function names
Triggers MCP sequential thinking for anything remotely complex

savage_analysis.verdict: The most over-engineered refactoring tool ever conceived. Uses more engineering to manage refactoring than most entire applications. It's architectural masturbation disguised as productivity.

complexity_analysis.claimed_benefit: Comprehensive codebase analysis with thinking-driven discovery

issues_identified: Reasonable approach but verbose in execution
Could be more direct in many cases
Thinking blocks add some value but create overhead

savage_analysis.verdict: Actually the most reasonable command in this collection. Still has thinking-block bloat but at least delivers value proportional to its complexity.
